SEARCH
Package de.ddb.labs.timeparser

Class TimeParser

java.lang.Object
de.ddb.labs.timeparser.TimeParser
public final class TimeParser extends Object

Converts a textual representation of a point or period in time into:

  • a value, that can be used to sort documents by (starting) date, and
  • a list of time periods in which it lies.

The process consists of four steps:

  1. Rule selection
  2. Input normalization
  3. Time span calculation
  4. Final output string generation

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes

    Class
    Description
    static enum 
    TimeParser.IndexDaysMode
    Output day-index strategy used by parseTime(...).

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    String
    applyNormalizationRules(String input)
    Applies all entries from normalizations.csv to the raw input (step 1 of the pipeline).
    String

    Applies a selected rule to the raw input and produces the normalized parser expression (step 4 of the pipeline).
    String
    buildFacetString(List<FacetNotation> facetNotations)
    Builds the pipe-separated facet id string from a list of notations (step 5b of the pipeline).
    long
    computeIndexDay(LocalDate date, TimeParser.IndexDaysMode indexDaysMode)
    Converts a calendar date to a sortable index day number (step 6 of the pipeline).

    Finds all rules whose input mask matches the given tokenized input (step 3 of the pipeline).

  • Method Details

    • getInstance

      public static TimeParser getInstance()
      Returns the shared parser instance.
      Returns:
      singleton parser instance

    • getRules

      public List<Rule> getRules()
      Returns all loaded transformation rules.
      Returns:
      loaded rules

    • getErrorStats

      public Map<String,ParseErrorStats> getErrorStats()
      Returns a snapshot of aggregated parse error counters.
      Returns:
      immutable map keyed by error type

    • resetErrorStats

      public void resetErrorStats()
      Clears all aggregated parse error counters.

    • parseTime

      public String parseTime(String input)
      Parses input using default settings (no context id, Julian Day output index).
      Parameters:
      input - textual date/time expression
      Returns:
      parsed output or empty string on parse failure

    • parseTime

      public String parseTime(String input, TimeParser.IndexDaysMode indexDaysMode)
      Parses input with an explicit day-index mode and no context id.
      Parameters:
      input - textual date/time expression
      indexDaysMode - output day-index strategy
      Returns:
      parsed output or empty string on parse failure

    • parseTime

      public String parseTime(String input, String contextId)
      Parses input with optional context id using Julian Day output index.
      Parameters:
      input - textual date/time expression
      contextId - optional correlation id for logging/monitoring
      Returns:
      parsed output or empty string on parse failure

    • parseTime

      public String parseTime(String input, String contextId, TimeParser.IndexDaysMode indexDaysMode)
      Parses input with optional context id and explicit day-index mode.
      Parameters:
      input - textual date/time expression
      contextId - optional correlation id for logging/monitoring
      indexDaysMode - output day-index strategy
      Returns:
      parsed output or empty string on parse failure

    • parseTimeResult

      public ParseResult parseTimeResult(String input)
      Parses input and returns the full structured result instead of only the legacy output string.
      Parameters:
      input - textual date/time expression
      Returns:
      structured parse result that can be serialized as JSON

    • parseTimeResult

      public ParseResult parseTimeResult(String input, TimeParser.IndexDaysMode indexDaysMode)
      Parses input into a structured result with an explicit day-index mode.

    • parseTimeResult

      public ParseResult parseTimeResult(String input, String contextId)
      Parses input into a structured result with an optional context id.

    • parseTimeResult

      public ParseResult parseTimeResult(String input, String contextId, TimeParser.IndexDaysMode indexDaysMode)
      Parses input into a structured result with optional context id and explicit day-index mode.

    • applyRule

      public String applyRule(String input, Rule rule) throws Exception
      Applies a selected rule to the raw input and produces the normalized parser expression (step 4 of the pipeline).
      Parameters:
      input - raw or pre-normalized input string
      rule - the rule to apply
      Returns:
      transformed input ready for TimeSpanParser
      Throws:
      Exception

    • findMatchingRules

      public List<Rule> findMatchingRules(String tokenizedInput)
      Finds all rules whose input mask matches the given tokenized input (step 3 of the pipeline). The input must already have month/weekday names replaced by their tokens (see tokenizeMonthsAndWeekdays(String)).
      Parameters:
      tokenizedInput - normalized input with month/weekday tokens
      Returns:
      matching rules; empty list if none match, more than one indicates an ambiguity

    • tokenizeMonthsAndWeekdays

      public String tokenizeMonthsAndWeekdays(String preprocessedInput)
      Replaces month and weekday names with cheap matching tokens (step 2 of the pipeline). Call this after applyNormalizationRules(String).
      Parameters:
      preprocessedInput - output of applyNormalizationRules(String)
      Returns:
      input with month names replaced by MM and weekday names by GG

    • applyNormalizationRules

      public String applyNormalizationRules(String input)
      Applies all entries from normalizations.csv to the raw input (step 1 of the pipeline). This expands abbreviations, canonical spelling variants, and century/millennium expressions before rule matching.
      Parameters:
      input - raw input string
      Returns:
      pre-normalized input ready for tokenizeMonthsAndWeekdays(String)

    • resolveFacetNotations

      public List<FacetNotation> resolveFacetNotations(TimeSpan timeSpan)
      Looks up all DDB Zeitvokabular facets that overlap with the given time span (step 5 of the pipeline).
      Parameters:
      timeSpan - the parsed time span
      Returns:
      matching facet notations in chronological order

    • buildFacetString

      public String buildFacetString(List<FacetNotation> facetNotations)
      Builds the pipe-separated facet id string from a list of notations (step 5b of the pipeline).
      Parameters:
      facetNotations - result of resolveFacetNotations(TimeSpan)
      Returns:
      e.g. "time_62100|time_62110", or empty string if the list is empty

    • computeIndexDay

      public long computeIndexDay(LocalDate date, TimeParser.IndexDaysMode indexDaysMode)
      Converts a calendar date to a sortable index day number (step 6 of the pipeline).
      Parameters:
      date - the start or end date of a time span
      indexDaysMode - TimeParser.IndexDaysMode.JULIAN_DAY (default) or TimeParser.IndexDaysMode.LEGACY
      Returns:
      sortable day number
